name: pyobjc-framework-coreservices

metadata.name: pyobjc-framework-CoreServices
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Wrappers for the framework CoreServices on macOS

documentation:
Wrappers for the "CoreServices" framework on macOS.

These wrappers don't include documentation, please check Apple's documentation
for information on how to use this framework and PyObjC's documentation
for general tips and tricks regarding the translation between Python
and (Objective-)C frameworks


Project links
-------------

* `Documentation <https://pyobjc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/>`_

* `Issue Tracker <https://github.com/ronaldoussoren/pyobjc/issues>`_

* `Repository <https://github.com/ronaldoussoren/pyobjc/>`_

modules.name: Lib.CoreServices.SearchKit
documentation:
Python mapping for the SearchKit framework.

This module does not contain docstrings for the wrapped code, check Apple's
documentation for details on how to use these functions and classes.

functions.name: Lib.CoreServices.SearchKit._workaround
documentation: SKIndexGetTypeID is documented, but not actually exported by Leopard. Try to
emulate the missing functionality.
See also radar:6525606.
UPDATE 20151123: The workaround is still necessary on OSX 10.11

modules.name: Lib.CoreServices
documentation:
Python mapping for the CoreServices framework.

This module does not contain docstrings for the wrapped code, check Apple's
documentation for details on how to use these functions and classes.

Note that PyObjC only wrappers the non-deprecated parts of the CoreServices
framework.

modules.name: pyobjc_setup
documentation:
Generic setup.py file for PyObjC framework wrappers.

This file should only be changed in pyobjc-core and then copied
to all framework wrappers.

To change this file:
    - Change the copy in pyobjc-core/Tools/pyobjc_setup.py
    - Run development-support/update-shared-files

functions.name: pyobjc_setup.Extension
documentation:
Simple wrapper about distutils.core.Extension that adds additional PyObjC
specific flags.

functions.name: pyobjc_setup.recursiveGlob
documentation:
Recursively look for files matching 'pathPattern'. Return a list
of matching files/directories.

functions.name: pyobjc_setup.importExternalTestCases
documentation: Import all unittests in the PyObjC tree starting at 'root'
